title: First Floor
subtitle: Circulation, Genre Cubbies, Special Collections, and The Mill
tour_intro: Welcome to the first floor — the library's most active level. This tour visits seven key service points and learning spaces, from the Circulation Desk where you'll check out materials, through specialized resources like The Studio and The Mill makerspace.

title: Second Floor
subtitle: Research Help Desk, Magazines & Journals, DVDs, Travel Guides, Drop-In Tutoring, and the Writing Center
tour_intro: The second floor is the library's research and academic support hub. This tour walks through six stops including the Research Help Desk, Vandal Drop-In Tutoring, the Writing Center, the periodical stacks, and the Center for Digital Inquiry and Learning.

title: Third Floor
subtitle: Quiet Study Floor — Oversize Books A-Z, Books H-S, CDs, LPs, and Microforms
tour_intro: The third floor is a quiet study floor housing the bulk of the library's main book collection (call numbers H–S), oversize books, and audio/visual materials. Tour stops cover the microforms, group and individual study spaces, and the main stacks.

title: Fourth Floor
subtitle: Quiet Study Floor — Individual Study Areas, Books A-G
tour_intro: The fourth floor is the library's quietest study level. Books A–G live here, along with graduate student carrels, individual study spaces, and specialized resources including the Boise Cascade Assistive Technology Room and Research Computing and Data Services.
